frameworks.name: Bayesian reinforcement learning
frameworks.one_line: Prediction errors modulated by probabilistic beliefs over cue-outcome relations

frameworks.name: reward prediction error hypothesis
frameworks.one_line: Phasic dopamine as RPE, extended to accommodate Bayesian belief updating

frameworks.name: adaptive coding
frameworks.one_line: Dopamine prediction errors rescale with the range of reward magnitudes

frameworks.name: recurrent cortical dynamics
frameworks.one_line: Orbitofrontal cortex transforms stimulus representations via recurrent dynamics

core_question: Can dopamine response anomalies be explained by Bayesian inference over cue-outcome relationships?
key_assumptions: Phasic dopamine reports a prediction error signal
Probabilistic beliefs about cue-outcome contingencies are updated by Bayes rule
Orbitofrontal cortex provides the transformed state representation
manipulations: Sensory preconditioning paradigms inferring value from cue chains
Reward magnitudes varied across orders of magnitude
key_findings: Dopamine responses track inferred value in sensory preconditioning
Cue pre-exposure produces latent inhibition effects in dopamine signaling
Prediction errors are adaptively coded across reward magnitudes
interpretations: Phasic dopamine implements a Bayesian RL update over inferred states, not raw RPE
OFC recurrent dynamics transform inputs so a simple error rule realizes Bayesian RL
